title: 👑 Elite Challenge ⭐⭐⭐⭐: Reading Comprehension: Everyday Texts

questions.prompt:
Read the text:
"Maya moved to a new city for work. At first, she felt lonely — she didn't know anyone and spent most evenings at home. But after a few weeks, she joined a running club. Soon she had friends, and the city started to feel like home."

Which sentence best describes Maya's experience?

options.text: Maya felt lonely at first but then made friends.
Maya was happy from the moment she arrived.
Maya never felt comfortable in the new city.
Maya moved back to her old city after a few weeks.

questions.explanation: The story has two stages: lonely at first → found friends through the running club. (b) is wrong — she was lonely at first. (c) is wrong — the city "started to feel like home" at the end. (d) is not mentioned — she stayed and settled in.

questions.prompt:
Read the email:
"Hi team,
The Monday meeting is moved to Wednesday this week — same time (10 am), same room (B12).
Also, please send your weekly reports by Tuesday noon instead of Wednesday.
See you then,
Karim"

Which TWO things have changed according to the email? Choose the most complete answer.

options.text: The meeting time and the room number.
The meeting day and the report deadline.
The meeting room and the report deadline.
The meeting day and the meeting time.

questions.explanation: Two things changed: (1) the meeting moved from Monday to Wednesday (the day changed, not the time or room), and (2) the report deadline moved from Wednesday to Tuesday noon. Time and room are the same. (a) and (c) include room — wrong, it is still B12. (d) the time did not change.

questions.prompt:
Read the story:
"The old theatre had been empty for twenty years. Many people thought it should be demolished and replaced with a car park. But a group of young artists disagreed. They raised money, repaired the building, and reopened it last spring. Now it hosts weekly shows and workshops."

What does "demolished" most likely mean?

options.text: Decorated.
Sold.
Destroyed.
Closed.

questions.explanation: Context clue: demolished and replaced with a car park — the idea is to remove the building entirely and put something else in its place. That meaning is destroyed. Decorated and sold would not require replacing with a car park. Closed is what happened already (it was empty).

questions.prompt:
Read the story:
"The old theatre had been empty for twenty years. Many people thought it should be demolished and replaced with a car park. But a group of young artists disagreed. They raised money, repaired the building, and reopened it last spring. Now it hosts weekly shows and workshops."

Which sentence is TRUE?

options.text: Everyone in the community wanted to demolish the theatre.
The theatre has been empty for ten years.
The theatre now has a car park next to it.
The artists raised money to save and repair the theatre.

questions.explanation: The text says the artists "raised money, repaired the building, and reopened it" — (d) is true. (a) is false — "many people" wanted demolition, but the artists disagreed. (b) is false — it was empty for twenty years. (c) the car park was the alternative that was NOT built.

questions.prompt:
Read the notice:
"TRAIN SERVICE UPDATE
Due to engineering works, all trains between Central and Northfield will be replaced by buses on Saturday and Sunday.
Journey time will be approximately 45 minutes longer than normal.
Tickets remain valid."

A passenger has a train ticket for Sunday from Central to Northfield. What should they expect?

options.text: Their ticket is not valid and they must buy a bus ticket.
They will take a bus, which will take about 45 minutes longer.
The train will run on time on Sunday.
They cannot travel on Sunday.

questions.explanation: The notice says trains are replaced by buses on Saturday and Sunday, the journey takes about 45 minutes longer, and "tickets remain valid." So the passenger takes a bus (not a train), the ticket is still valid (not invalid or needing to buy a new one), and travel is still possible.

questions.prompt:
Read the email:
"Dear parents,
We are delighted to inform you that our school won first place in the regional science competition.
The winning team — Lina, Omar, and Sana — will receive their certificates at a special assembly on Friday at 2 pm.
All parents are welcome to attend.
Mrs Hamdi, Principal"

Which sentence is NOT true according to the email?

options.text: Three students won the competition.
The assembly is on Friday at 2 pm.
Parents must register to attend the assembly.
The school came first in the regional competition.

questions.explanation: The email says "All parents are welcome to attend" — no registration is mentioned. (c) is not true (adding a condition not in the text). (a) Three students: Lina, Omar, and Sana ✓. (b) Assembly on Friday at 2 pm ✓. (d) First place in the regional competition ✓.
